title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.
Use fixed strings, basic or extended regular expressions, anchors, classes, context, and exit status with explicit locale assumptions.
Separate find traversal, predicates, grouping, pruning, printing, and actions while protecting path boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 3-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy. Competency probe: Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.

Inspect the Text Inspection trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.

content.text: For Text Inspection, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: cat is the safest viewer for every binary or unbounded file.
